---
title: "The G.I. Bill and Vocational Training"

status: "Job Market Paper"
year: 2026
order: 1

description: >
  This paper studies how the educational provisions of the World War II
  and Korean War G.I. Bills affected vocational training, educational
  attainment, and long-run labor-market outcomes.

categories:
  - Labor Economics
  - Economic History
  - Economics of Education

pdf: "../../files/papers/molligo-gi-bill-vocational.pdf"

# Uncomment these when the files or links exist:
# slides: "../../files/papers/gi-bill-vocational-slides.pdf"
# appendix: "../../files/papers/gi-bill-vocational-appendix.pdf"
# code: "https://github.com/pmolligo/example-repository"

citation:
  type: article
  author:
    - family: Molligo
      given: Patrick A.
  title: "The G.I. Bill and Vocational Training"
  issued: 2026
---

::: {.paper-page-links}

[Download PDF](../../files/papers/molligo-gi-bill-vocational.pdf){.btn .btn-primary}

<!-- Add these buttons when available:

[Slides](../../files/papers/gi-bill-vocational-slides.pdf){.btn .btn-outline-primary}

[Online appendix](../../files/papers/gi-bill-vocational-appendix.pdf){.btn .btn-outline-primary}

[Code](https://github.com/pmolligo/example-repository){.btn .btn-outline-primary}

-->

:::

## Abstract

The educational provisions of the World War II and Korean War G.I. Bills dramatically expanded access to postsecondary education and training. This paper examines their effects on vocational-school attendance and completion.

Replace this paragraph with the current abstract from the paper. A paper-page abstract can be longer than the short `description` used on the Research page.

## Results

Briefly summarize the principal findings here. This section is optional, but it can help visitors understand the paper without opening the PDF.

## Presentations

- Conference or seminar, year
- Conference or seminar, year

## Citation

Molligo, Patrick A. “The G.I. Bill and Vocational Training.” Working paper, 2026.
